capacity_asymmetry_band.statement: AR3 is testable: a capacity asymmetry moves the selected scale off the midpoint by a computable amount, and the three-loop implied scale measures it.

claim_boundary: The implication from AR1, AR2, AR3 to the midpoint scale is proved exactly. The premises are a proposed selection principle without a derivation from the five axioms; the theorem shares the claim class of the completed conditional laws and inherits the registered three-loop discriminating test.

AR1_reconciliation.needed: derivation from the record/repair structure of the axioms
AR1_reconciliation.statement: the criticality boundary reconciles the gauge-unification record and the transmutation record

AR2_quadratic_cost.needed: promotion of the repair-calculus quadratic mismatch form to this record class
AR2_quadratic_cost.statement: reconciliation cost is quadratic in renormalization time offset

AR3_equal_capacity.needed: a capacity computation for both anchor records; the three-loop implied scale measures any asymmetry
AR3_equal_capacity.statement: the two anchor records carry equal capacity

theorem.conclusion: the boundary scale is the log-midpoint sqrt(mu_U * E_cell) = E_star exp(-pi) P^(-1/6); with unequal capacities it is the capacity-weighted geometric mean
theorem.name: ANCHOR_RECONCILIATION_MIDPOINT_SELECTION_THEOREM
theorem.proof: the cost is strictly convex in renormalization time with positive second derivative 2(w1+w2); the exact gradient vanishes only at the weighted mean; equal weights give the midpoint; exponent algebra gives the closed form
